food_subgroup: Vietnamese bread-based skillet meal with meat, egg, and pâté

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn term exists for Bánh Mì Chảo. FOODON_03315595 (sandwich food product) is the closest parent covering bread-based composite meals with meat and egg fillings.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP (bread-based composite dishes) is the closest defensible parent. No exact code exists for Vietnamese skillet bánh mì.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code found for Bánh Mì Chảo or a sufficiently close parent food concept.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving of Bánh Mì Chảo is approximately 350 g, comprising a split baguette served in a hot skillet with ham, egg, pâté, sausage, and fish.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated composite nutrient profile for Bánh Mì Chảo based on USDA FoodData Central component ingredients: French bread, ham, egg (fried), pork liver pâté, pork sausage, and fish (tilapia, cooked)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (Foundation Foods and SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for Bánh Mì Chảo exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA FoodData Central entries for the declared ingredients: French baguette (~40% by weight), ham (~15%), fried egg (~15%), pork liver pâté (~10%), pork sausage (~10%), and cooked fish (~10%). Proportions are representative of a typical restaurant serving. Values are per 100 g of the composite dish.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of white bread and composite bread-based meals — analogous estimate based on French baguette GI data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Bánh Mì Chảo. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for white French baguette (GI ~70) adjusted downward to ~65 to account for the protein, fat, and pâté components that slow gastric emptying and blunt the glycemic response. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 71.75 g carbohydrate in 350 g serving) / 100 ≈ 46.6, rounded to 46.8.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~620 mg per 100 g; ~2170 mg per serving) from processed ham, sausage, pâté, and fish
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid Bánh Mì Chảo due to its very high sodium load from multiple processed meat components; a single serving may exceed the WHO daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (~65) driven by refined white baguette, partially offset by protein and fat from egg, ham, and pâté
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume Bánh Mì Chảo in moderation; the high glycemic load per serving (~47) can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation, and portion control or substitution with a wholegrain bread base is advisable.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Combination of high sodium, saturated fat from pâté and sausage, and dietary cholesterol from egg and processed meats
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit Bánh Mì Chảo due to its high sodium content, moderate saturated fat (~2.8 g per 100 g), and cholesterol (~115 mg per 100 g), all of which are associated with elevated cardiovascular risk when consumed regularly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bánh Mì Chảo is a Vietnamese skillet bread dish in which a split French baguette is served hot in a cast-iron or metal skillet alongside a combination of pan-fried or griddled toppings that typically include sliced ham, a fried egg, Vietnamese pork liver pâté (gan xay), pork sausage (chả lụa or xúc xích), and fish. The dish is a hearty, savoury breakfast or brunch item popular in Vietnam and found in Vietnamese restaurants across Singapore. The baguette, a legacy of French colonial influence on Vietnamese cuisine, is crusty on the outside and soft inside, and is used to scoop up or wrap the skillet-cooked toppings. The combination of ingredients delivers a rich umami flavour profile with contrasting textures — crispy bread crust, silky egg yolk, smooth pâté, and firm sausage. Nutritionally, the dish is energy-dense (~198 kcal per 100 g) with moderate protein (~10.8 g), moderate fat (~8.5 g), and a significant carbohydrate contribution from the baguette (~20.5 g per 100 g). Sodium content is notably high (~620 mg per 100 g) due to the multiple processed meat components, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index is estimated at 65 (medium), and the glycemic load per full serving is high (~47), which is relevant for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those with gluten or fish allergies.

confidence_reason: No direct entry for Bánh Mì Chảo exists in any primary regional approved source (HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, IMR MyFCD, FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA FoodData Central component ingredient entries. GI is an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for white French baguette, adjusted for the mixed macronutrient composition of the dish. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted ingredient composite using USDA FoodData Central (Foundation Foods / SR Legacy) as no direct database entry exists for Bánh Mì Chảo in any approved primary regional source. Ingredient weight proportions assumed: baguette 40%, ham 15%, fried egg 15%, pork liver pâté 10%, pork sausage 10%, cooked fish 10%. 2. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on white French baguette GI (~70, University of Sydney GI Database), adjusted downward for the protein and fat content of the composite dish. 3. Sodium value (~620 mg per 100 g) reflects the high sodium load from multiple processed meat components and should be treated as an estimate; actual restaurant sodium may vary. 4. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data and are not directly measured. 5. The dish is classified as Vietnamese cuisine served in a Singapore restaurant context; multilingual names are provided for English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi, and Vietnamese per the Singaporean cuisine multilingual rule. 6. Halal status is listed as false as pork-derived ingredients (pork sausage, pork liver pâté) are present. 7. FoodOn match FOODON_03315595 and FoodEx2 code A0FJP are closest-parent matches only; no exact ontology code exists for this dish.
